retry_reason: kref_put guarantees that the release function is called exactly once when the refcount reaches zero; retrying would cause a double-free.
contract_summary: This function is the kref release callback for batadv_orig_node. It releases all references held by the originator node (neighbor nodes, interface info, VLAN entries, multicast state) and then schedules RCU callbacks for final memory deallocation. It is invoked automatically by kref_put when the last reference is dropped. The caller must not access the orig_node after this function runs.

judge.reason: The function's sole purpose is to release all resources held by an orig_node when its kref count reaches zero. All operations (hlist iteration, sub-object puts, mcast purge) are part of the cleanup process. No unrelated business logic exists.

check.reason: All four put operations are present in the code: the function iterates over neigh_list, ifinfo_list, and vlan_list, calling the respective put function on each node, and also calls batadv_orig_ifinfo_put on last_bonding_candidate. The member access paths (e.g., neigh_node.refcount.refcount) and type chains (e.g., batadv_neigh_node--kref--refcount_t) are consistent with the callee information, and the segment counts match (3 segments, 3 types). The location 'global' indicates the local variables, which is appropriate as these objects are obtained from list traversal or assignment and are not function parameters or return values.
